entry.title: How to Reclaim size of volume "/hana/shared and /hana/log" when it is full.
Re: New or not to the SAP Community, share your story!
entry.content:
Hello everyone!

I'm Louay! Just wanted to share a little about myself. I've graduated from the SAP YPP 2023 here in Yemen. It's been an amazing journey, and I've chosen to specialize in the PP module.

I am excited to be a part of this vibrant community and look forward to learning, sharing, and growing together. I believe that the SAP community is a treasure trove of knowledge and experience, and I am eager to tap into this resource.

If you've got any advice or tips on how to get the most out of this community, or anything related to the PP module, I'm all ears. I'd really appreciate it.

Cheers,

entry.title: Re: if end if inside the case end -case
entry.content: I am using DATS data type.(it will capture the sy-datum value in it as per logic)....(added new image ) .Is there any way I can write validation inside CASE ENDCASE. I have to check if the value is available or not .

entry.title: Limit machine capacity consumption per customer
entry.content:
Hi all,

I have a requirement to limit the maximum machine consumed capacity by a specific customer. We have implemented the capacity reservations but this is diferent because we don't want to book some hours per cusomter, we need to limit the maximum capacity consumed.

Is it possible?

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: OData V2 Request fails and no error message is displayed
entry.content: Do you have access to /n/IWFND/GW_CLIENT to make some requests and test the requests?

entry.title: Re: New Bank Accounts don't appear in manual bank statement?
entry.content:
Hi,

 

there are already really good blogs out there and a lot of content. Please read and follow this for instance:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/bank-account-management-in-sap-s-4hana/ba-p/13448994

 

 

Best,

Edrilan Berisha

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Financials Development

entry.title: Can we add password authentication to Sap Application Studio Solutions?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Can we add password authentication to Sap Application Studio Solutions?

We would like to create a Multi Customer Solution and would like to ask if we can put a password authentication to it so that no one can see the source code / scripts once deployed in different tenants?

Is it also possible in Customer Specific Solutions?

 

Thank you and Regards,

Carlo

entry.title: Answer text editor strange behavior
entry.content:
Hi,

Every once in a while the text editor, for answering a question, will start behaving strangely.

For example, while typing the caret may jump to the beginning of the line, and everything typed, will continue from there. Placing the caret back at the end of the line, does not help. Press any key, and the caret will jump to the start of the line again. If you place the caret anywhere before the last letter in that line, you can type normally.

Another phenomenon is some sort of weird autocorrect. The following screenshot shows this. Here I was trying to type "Regards,", when I noticed that the editor had all of a sudden changed the text:

 

On the few occasions that this happens, closing and reopening the page and/or browser, will solve the problem.

Regards,

Johan

entry.title: Re: Can i create 1 physical inventory document for more t...
entry.content: To implement WM or EWM for that matter just to simply storage location structure is killing a fly with a sledgehammer, is it not?

entry.title: Re: Answer text editor strange behavior
entry.content: Perhaps of interest, I had just used the browser's translate function on the question.

entry.title: IDOC Metadata Error
entry.content:
Hi Experts,
Im new to SAP PO, need your help with "IDOC_ERROR_METADATA_UNAVAILABLE" issue.
In my case 3 types of IDOC transfers are happening from X system to Y system via SAP PO,
DESADV.DELVRY03, INVOIC.INVOIC01 and ORDRSP.ORDERS05.
Issue only occurs with the ORDRSP.ORDERS05 IDOC, Fails in SM58 with Metadata error.
For all 3 IDOC's same channel is used, tried using different channel but no luck.

I tried uploading the metadata manually but even in IDOC metadata monitor im facing the issue with data mismatch, i.e.
When i upload the metadata with details(sender channel) of system X, once uploaded its displayed as system Y in repository ID. The sender channel of Repository ID X is in Repository ID Y.

When i used receiver channel for the metadata upload it gets uploaded but the sender channel is not available.

entry.title: Re: Extensibility options for Adobe Livecycle Designer in Business ByDesign
entry.content: @dieter_haensch2 and/or @leonardo_felini, are these webinars still running? I am working with ALCD for ByD and keen to upskill and stay updated on new features and developments.

entry.title: Re: if end if inside the case end -case
entry.content: You're going in the wrong direction. You should not look at the table in SE11, but instead look at the variable type in the debugger.

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Upgrade Faster – Managing app lifecycle impacts on users
entry.content: Hi @marcel_schifferle  re keeping up with the pace of development at SAP ... that's what the Clean Core story is all about, i.e. guidance on choosing upgrade-safe ways to run and extend your solution so you can upgrade regularly, quickly, and smoothly.

entry.title: Re: Replication of Valid User name from SF EC to SAP S/4 or SAP ERP in case of Multiple Employments
entry.content:
Hi Vamshi, 

It seems, screen dumps of the source code not allowed to share. Please schedule a google meeting so that I will explain how to write the piece of code.

entry.title: Re: S4HC - Consume Local events
entry.content:
@vkesarir ,

Thanks for the question. 

Did you get a chance to have a look at the Inbound Delivery Change event : https://api.sap.com/event/CE_INBOUNDDELIVERYEVENTS/overview 

You can generate the event consumption model in ADT - Ecplise which has some of the artifacts like Inbound Services; Behavioural Defnitions (BDEF); Classes and event interfaces. I think you can use the the business events via Enterprise event handiling locally.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/8cbf952e55364254be2da77aa1342aa5.html 

But if you wanted to consume Business events to inform to the consumer then you should subscribe to the SAP Event Mesh. 

Hope this helps, Otherwise please let us know. 

Best Regards,

Sri

entry.title: Re: Answer text editor strange behavior
entry.content: Just double checked, and it appears that translating a page with the browser's built-in function, will cause both behaviors. Once used, all entered text will immediately be translated. Apparently "rega" translates to "water" in Portuguese.

entry.title: Re: Answer text editor strange behavior
entry.content: Possibly a Windows feature, completely unrelated to SAP = Settings > Time & Language > Typing > Autocorrect misspelled words:

entry.title: Re: Fiori elements app shows wrong decimal separators
entry.content: Thank you for your update, really helpfull.

entry.title: Re: Withholding Tax Type for Invoice Posting
entry.content: I have added the tag "SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Finance", as this topic seems to be Finance related.

entry.title: Where can I learn how to create master data for manufacturing and production using SAP S/4HANA?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Exploring Basic Data for Manufacturing and Product Management in SAP S/4HANA learning journey

Overview
In this course you will gain a comprehensive understanding of creating master data for manufacturing and production using SAP S/4HANA.

Learning objectives
After completing this course, consultants and business users will be able to:

  • Create materials, material BOMs, work centers and routings for manufacturing and production
  • Assign documents to master data
  • Perform a classification
  • Use the engineering change management
  • Use advanced functions of bill of materials
  • Use advanced functions of routings

Prerequisites
S4200 - Business Processes in SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! 
We are here to support you. I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: SAPUI5 Flexibility: Adapting UIs for Specific User Roles
entry.content:
@RaminS 

Could you provide a bit more context? What's your concern with the links? Depending on your reasons, there might be different solutions.

1. Is the list of links too long, and you're aiming for a cleaner UI? If so, there's an option within the key user adaptation 'Adapt UI'. You can click on "More Links" and remove any unwanted links from the list. However, keep in mind that each end user can add those links back, and you can't remove the links permanently. It's more of a "hide" feature.
2. Or, are you worried about security and don't want to give your end users access to those apps? In that case, your FLP administrator can easily remove them from your catalog. Then, the links should automatically disappear and you do not need to use 'Adapt UI'.

entry.title: Download CSV file
entry.content: Hello builders , i just want to know if i can download a CSV file (empty) from the sap build apps .

entry.title: Re: New Bank Accounts don't appear in manual bank statement?

Has anybody faced the same issue earlier?????

Has the problem been resolved?

entry.title: Re: Getting access & accessing SAP Learning Hub #SAPEducation
entry.content:
Hi @longnguyen 
Please use our  SAP Learning Q&A board to post 

This makes it easier  to follow-up:

That way other learners can benefit from the answer:
If you ask the question there other learners  can search for it in the future if they run into the same issue and there we have more features available for responses

Would you mind to repost it?
Kind regards

Margit

entry.title: Re: Difference Between Asset Acquisition in F0859 and MIR...
entry.content:
With MIRO you do not need a reference PO. 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/b805ca4159884132a3799fa6a4a3ff85.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: Withholding Tax Type for Invoice Posting
entry.content:
Hello @Dario_Bustillos ,

 

I'd like to inform you that the SSCUI 104225 - "Define Withholding Tax Type for Invoice Posting" is only available in the country/region of BR, ES, ID, IN, IT, JP, MX, PH, PT, SG, TH, TR. Please kindly check the country/region.

Here is the resource for how to find above information: Kindly go to SAP Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition, select the version and country/region, select implementation tab in Accelerators section, and download attached file "Configuration apps".

See our SAP note for further information: 3384280 - Activating Extended Withholding Tax - S/4HANA Cloud

 

I hope this helps, in case you have further questions you can update this thread.

 

Best regards,
Tamas

entry.title: Re: Freight Unit is not created automatically for Sales O...
entry.content:
Hello @Krish01 

  1. Check TM integration settings with /SCMTMS/CHK_DOC_INT_TM_INTEGR report.
  2. Check if automatic freight unit building is active in the logistics integration profile. If it is not, freight units need to be created with /SCMTMS/SUBSEQUENT_FUB_LOGINT report.
  3. Make sure that your sales order is complete and not blocked.
  4. Check if integration based on actual quantities is active in the logistics integration profile. If it is, verify that the sales order quantity is confirmed by ATP.
  5. Try to create freight units for the sales order with /SCMTMS/SUBSEQUENT_FUB_LOGINT report and check what the log says.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Is there any possible way to do a calculation in SAC using two different measures from diff models ?
entry.content: SAC Calculation = measure1 (Model 01) / measure2 (Model 02)

entry.title: 10+ ways to reshape your SAP land-scape with SAP Business Technology - Blog 9
entry.content:
This blog is part of the “10+ ways to reshape your SAP landscape with SAP Business Technology Platform Blog Series”

Summary

In this blog series we will look at the role of the SAP Business Technology Platform to reshape your SAP landscape including SAP and Non-SAP systems. In this blog we discuss SAP Business Technology Platform capabilities around integrated planning. Bringing together manually entered or automatically generated plan data with transactional data, mostly from S/4HANA, is a prerequisite for a truly integrated steering system.

 

Corporate planning is the central process for coordinating goals and resources over a fixed time horizon. However, disruptive events such as the pandemic or supply chain bottlenecks disturb this process. Even in turbulent times, it is crucial to structure the future directions. Through coordinated planning, top down targets should be set, activities and budgets created. In bigger companies, hundreds if not thousands of people are involved in such a process. A key element is flexibility without disregarding governance.

Supporting the planning process is a central IT topic. After all, digitalization means more than just entering and consolidating planning values in standalone tools or even worse in spreadsheets. Disparate planning systems make the life of controllers hard. The possibilities offered by modern information systems should be utilized to improve planning quality, increase flexibility and reduce costs. But what is a good planning architecture? This is not an entirely new topic, but one that is constantly being discussed.

Integrated (or extended) planning & analysis is an important aspect of financial transformation. Planning usually takes place at different IT levels. These levels are somewhat simplified: operational transaction systems (e.g. ERP), data warehouses and systems for analysis. However, planning is often only seen as a sub-aspect of analysis. This is not necessarily wrong, but it is too simplified, as will be explained later.

A deeper look at planning reveals that there are at least two basic planning streams: planning that is usually orchestrated bycontrolling. It is profit-driven, and it is not only focussed on value flows, but also works with (usually aggregated) drivers. A typical planning method is cost center planning based on reference values.

But there are also many operationally oriented plannings, which are closely linked to execution. The focus is on the coordination of operational service creation processes. But secondary processes such as human resources are also supported. Individual objectives dominate here. For example, feasibility is an important goal in production planning. Efficiency is of course also essential here, but only partially. For example, liquidity is usually not considered.

Gartner has already pointed out in 2020 that the existing integration at many companies is not satisfactory, not only conceptually but also in terms of IT. The essence of so called xP&A (extended planning and analytics) is end-to-end planning in order to avoid wrong decisions due to a lack of coordination. For instance, adjustments in production have always consequences on profit, on balance sheet and on the cash flow. The latter is not obvious but even a delay in production may result in a latter payment. This end-to-end approach is discussed in theory for many years. It is however rarely implemented from end to end.

Unfortunately, such an approach often fails due to the high level of complexity. It is therefore not surprising that the "right" IT architecture is of central importance here and is indispensable for overcoming complexity.

Who can be the driver of such integration? Controlling has to coordinate a complex planning process that includes medium-term planning, budgets and forecasts. The controller's toolbox is rather simple. In addition to spreadsheets, stand-alone planning solutions have been established and became independent. These are occasionally also integrated with other financial applications such as consolidation. This planning process is usually independent from operational systems and is often rather loosely linked to data warehouse or ERP. Metadata is usually synchronized at certain milestones with the operational systems, very often not automatically and sometimes even based on file exchange.

However, the more "strategic" the planning process becomes, the less the individual planning approach fits into a predefined framework, which planning also needs. This isone reason why spreadsheets still play such a strong role in medium-term planning (3-5 year horizon). The requirement of individuality is the reason why it has been used in planning for so long. However, modern planning systems are now filling this space. This brings at least two temporal levels closer together.

But what about operational planning and control? ERP is the basis of such planning. Operational planning systems arose from the need for operational control of ERP. Last but not least, the name "Enterprise Resource Planning" already indicates a planning focus. The integration of execution control takes a central role here.

Integration is of central importance in a planning process. true xP&A brings financial planning together with operational planning. Here are a few examples of key links:

  • A starting point of HR Planning is an extrapolation of all operational activities. Some of them are already future oriented like already known absences like pregnancy leave or sabbaticals. Additionally, employees are planned in operational personnel planning. Transfers, redundancies, part-time work and the necessary cost consequences must be planned and later executed in the HR system.
  • Integrated planning from a short-term perspective requires components of production planning, whereby most approaches usually involve rough-cut planning.
  • Planned measures, for example from strategic planning, are transformed into projects, which are then refined using project planning and execution.
  • Sales prices and sales measures are planned in the sales planning. But new products (as part of overall planning) must be available and sometimes created with an urgency in sales planning, for example.
  • Investment ideas emerge from the planning process. These must be evaluated. This is followed by a release process. Finally, the results must be transferred to asset management (asset accounting, maintenance planning). Also, all activities from existing investments must be accurately reflected in an integrated planning system. Even depreciations result into cash consequences though influencing tax calculation.

The sub-plans also need to be coordinated with each other. Employee planning should arise from capacity considerations resulting from the primary processes. Investment decisions result from capacity restrictions.

The advantages of such close integration are obvious:

  • Coordination improves so that there are no longer multiple "truths" or (temporary) inconsistencies.
  • Comprehensive scenarios are becoming increasingly important. Rapid countermeasures also mean that the consequences of changes must be visualized quickly and as fully as possible.
  • Duplicate planning is avoided. This also improves coordination.

Many modern planning systems like SAP Analytics Cloud have a generic structure so that the special requirements of operational planning approaches can be covered relatively easily. After all, personnel master data, work schedules or parts lists are simple data structures. But an extension is not that simple after all. This is because operational planning requires a close connection to the execution control system. Here are a few examples:

  • Production planning needs a close connection to scheduling. Current feedback influences short-term planning.
  • also in the future) must be taken into account and lead to the adjustment of (planned) capacities.
  • Personnel cost planning requires information from personnel development, which is a key function of Human Capital Management systems.
  • Budgets should be planned against current expenditure. Under certain circumstances, a budget freeze should take effect.
  • The short-term liquidity forecast is traditionally closely linked to accounting and is mapped accordingly in ERP. Medium-term liquidity planning is rather derived from profit and loss and financial planning. The medium-term view nevertheless requires the transitions from liquidity management. This requires a close connection to cash management. Very recent payment delays, for example, influence the liquidity forecast of integrated profit and finance planning

In this respect, expanding financial planning solutions is not enough. The integration of sub-planning with ERP and other operational systems must be much closer. The integration between operational planning and financial planning, on the other hand, appears to be much simpler.

Accordingly, there are also operational sub-planning systems that have been established for some time and are optimized for operational coordination (e.g. SAP Integrated Business Planning with a close link to production control). This is not a typical financial domain. It needs special operations like bill of material explosion (usually a recursive processing) and production program optimization (usually operations research methods). However, the profit and financial planning managed by Controlling should easily be linked to such operational planning and used for joint scenarios. Program optimization not only needs capacities but also costs (or even more accurate contribution margins) to generate a feasible program with the highest profit.

Why are these plans not immediately mapped in ERP, i.e. extended by the corresponding planning function in S/4HANA? This approach has indeed been discussed. However, ERP systems are only capable of abstracting to a limited extent. Financial planning usually takes place at an aggregated level. These levels must remain freely definable. Planning on cost type groups, product lines and regions are just a few examples. And ERP systems are not good at flexible processes and sometimes rather spontaneous ideas. Planning also includes vision and customization. Scenarios are often based on changed structures.

The ideal solution is an approach that functions independently of the ERP solution, but still maintains a close connection to and is harmonized with operational systems, thus creating a true integrated planning system. What are the requirements for a solution that integrates ERP, operational planning and financial planning?

  • Automatic synchronizing of master data in the sense of meta data management with the possibility of additions in all sub-components
  • Real-time availability of actual figures
  • a generic planning interface (for example, the ACDOCP table in SAP S4/HANA)
  • Writing back the planning results in operational processes (again using the ACDOCP)
  • Methods which are already set up in operational system for process actual data should be easily reused in planning systems like allocations, group eliminations and many more (e.g. using the definitions of the universal allocation in S/4HANA also for plan data coming from SAP Analytics Cloud)..

SAP's xP&A strategy describes such an integrative view. The following illustration uses a few connections to show what such a connection can look like in the SAP environment.

 

SAP’s xP&A Scenario

Modern planning therefore requires an intelligent combination of transactional systems and planning systems with a high level of integration. This intense coupling of systems needs a strong technical foundation. This is the clear strength of SAP Analytics Cloud Planning in combination with S/4HANA, SAP IBP and so on. The systems can communicate directly via OData. Therefore the applications can be set up more or less independently, though based on common structures which can be also synchronized.

However, if planning is only limited to the financial aspects, a direct link might be ok. But enhancing the controlling system by operational planning requires a lot more data. The more heterogenous the environment, the bigger is the need to use a unification layer to provide a unique view of planning systems on their environment. Non-SAP data is quite often needed to be the foundation of planning decisions. Datasphere is built for that. Some of the advantages:

  • Additional data can be used and integrated to enrich the foundation of planning.
  • Complex planning calculation can be done based on the Datasphere foundation with the use of PaPM
  • Individual predictive analytics functions can be used beyond Smart Predict, natively with python and / or using PAL or APL.
  • SAP Analytics Cloud has a native connection to Datasphere. This year the possibility to plan directly on Datasphere structures within SAP Analytics Cloud will be released.

The following picture shows the art of the possible with SAP BTP infrastructure.

 

How SAP provides the breadth and depth of planning capabilities (Micheal Emerson)

Only a strong technological foundation can be the base of a effective xP&A scenario.

entry.title: Re: Answer text editor strange behavior
entry.content: Checked that, but it was all turned off. In my case, I use Edge. The jumping of the caret I have not been able to duplicate yet.

entry.title: Absence half day, leave request half day with start time and end time, day model break ?
entry.content:
Dear experts,

We are trying to set up a leave request using half day with clock times in Time Off successfactors.  The issue is how to get the break segment start and end time on the start date of the leave request so we can use them for the start time and the end time of the half day depending on the first half or the second half of the day ?

Example: working day model:  9h00 - 18h    with a non-paid break 12h-13h

1st case -> Employee leave request: first half day

Target wanted:    employee leave request start time = 9h00 (start time of day model)

                           employee leave request end time time = 12h00 (start time of break)

 1st case -> Employee leave request: second half day

Target:    employee leave request start time = 13h00 (end time of break)

               employee leave request end time time = 18h00 (end time day model)

Please feel free to post any help?

Many thanks.

entry.title: retire Fully Depreciated Asset by abavn
entry.content:
Hello SAP gurus,

I'm trying to scrap assets that are fully depreciated this year.

for exemple,

When I run abavn, the accumulated depreciation amount is not reflected by month, but all.

I think only 4 months of depreciation should be added, but why is all 12 months applied?

entry.title: Re: Configure Different Trust Configurations for the Same Identity Authentication Tenant (Azure AD A
entry.content:
Hi Imre,

As per the step3, can we create the certificate for more than 365 days?

Otherwise we will end up to create/renew the certificate for every one year.

Thanks,

Santhosh.

entry.title: Re: S4HC - Consume Local events
entry.content:
Hi Sri,

Thanks for your reply.

I am trying to consume events locally to call some custom follow on actions.  I am looking to consume these events locally but the inbound/outbound delivery events are non-RAP objects.  Can you please guide me how to generate event consumption model to consume these events locally without using SAP event mesh.

Thanks,

Vijay

entry.title: Quality Assurance Approval & Instant Forward for Transport Requests in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Introduction

Among the variety of features offered by SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition in their 3 System Landscape (3SL), 2 new features have been introduced to enhance the management and control of Transport Requests (TRs) in the Test tenant, focusing on ensuring the quality of these TRs.

3SL consists of 3 Instances viz. Development, Test and Production to develop, test and deploy various configurations and customisations to the Productive environment. These changes are transported via TRs to move the objects from one tenant to the other in a quality-assured and systematic way. You can find more details on 3SL and TR Management on the SAP Help Portal.

Quality Assurance Approval

With the launch of the “Quality Assurance Approval” (QAA) feature, the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Test tenant now enables users to set the Quality Status of their TRs to either “Approved” or “Rejected”. Firstly, the user needs to have the Administrator Business Role for them to be able to access this feature. To activate this feature, the user must select the QAA option using the Settings button in the Import Collection app.

Fig 1 - QAA under Settings

A pop-up appears for the user to switch the QAA toggle from OFF to ON and then Save. Once the user enables this feature, a new column named “Quality Status” is displayed in the Import Collection app.

Fig 2 – QAA Toggle Pop-up

Fig 3 - Quality Status column

As and when a new TR is transported to the Test tenant, Quality Status is always assigned as Open. The old TRs which have already been forwarded to the Production tenant will also have the Open status. However, no action is required to be performed on the old TRs. Below are a few important points to remember while working with the QAA feature-

  • The user can Approve/Reject a TR by providing a Note along with it, irrespective of the Import status.
  • TRs can be forwarded to the Production tenant only once they are Approved.
  • Open or Rejected status will not let the user forward the TR. However, if the QAA settings are off, then TRs will be forwarded as per the initial “Forward Automation” settings.
  • Also, it’s required for a user to approve all the SAP Central Business Configuration TRs to move it to the Production tenant. If the user tries to reject those TRs, an error message will appear stating- “Collection of type SAP Central Business Configuration can't be rejected.”

Fig 4 - Approve/Reject dropdown

Additionally, the QAA feature is also available in SAP Cloud ALM (CALM) as “Approve for Production” button. The user can assign a TR to their Feature app and consequently Approve the assigned TR using the mentioned button. As of now, the user can only Approve the TR via CALM and not Reject it.

Fig 5 - Approve for Production in CALM

Instant Forward

When a user imports a TR successfully into the Test tenant, the TR will then be forwarded and visible in the Production tenant as per the scheduled batch job frequency which runs every 30 minutes based on the user’s Forward Automation Settings. Hence, the “Instant Forward” feature enables the user to let their TRs get transported from the Test tenant to the Production tenant as soon as the TR is imported into the Test tenant. However, if QAA is On, then only the Approved TRs will be forwarded instantly. Whereas, if QAA is Off, then all transports will be forwarded immediately.

To activate this feature, the user needs to open the Forward Automation option using the Settings button available in the Import Collection app. Once the Instant Forward checkbox is selected, all other options will be removed from the Forward Automation Settings pop-up.

Fig 6 - Instant Forward in Forward Automation Settings

Let’s deep dive into how these two settings work together through the below table:

Quality Assurance Approval Toggle

Instant Forward Checkbox

Quality Status

Forward Status Observation

On

On

Open / Rejected

Not Forwarded

On

On

Approved

Forwarded instantly

On

Off

Open / Rejected

Not Forwarded

On

Off

Approved

Forwarded as per Forward Automation Settings / Manually

Off

On

NA

Forwarded instantly

Off

Off

NA

Forwarded as per Forward Automation Settings / Manually

However, if there is an inter-dependency between the TRs then the scenario plays out a bit differently. Let’s say there are 2 transports TR1 and TR2 where TR2 is dependent on TR1.

Quality Assurance Approval Toggle

Instant Forward Checkbox

TR1 Quality Status

TR2 Quality Status

TR1 Forward Status Observation

TR2 Forward Status Observation

On

On

Open / Rejected

Open / Rejected

Not Forwarded

Not Forwarded

On

On

Approved

Open / Rejected

Forwarded instantly

Not Forwarded

On

On

Open / Rejected

Approved

Not Forwarded

Forwarded instantly but will not be imported in Production tenant due to dependency

On

On

Approved

Approved

Forwarded instantly

Forwarded instantly

On

Off

Open / Rejected

Open / Rejected

Not Forwarded

Not Forwarded

On

Off

Approved

Open / Rejected

Forwarded as per Forward Automation Settings / Manually

Not Forwarded

On

Off

Open / Rejected

Approved

Not Forwarded

Not Forwarded as per Forward Automation Settings. On manually forwarding, user will get an error stating that TR1 is not Approved.

On

Off

Approved

Approved

Forwarded as per Automation Settings / Manually

Forwarded as per Forward Automation Settings. On manually forwarding before TR1, the user will be prompted to forward the TR1 along with TR2 due to dependency. However, user can forward only TR2 by removing TR1 as dependency (i.e., by switching off the toggle, refer Fig 6) but TR2 will not be imported into the Production tenant until TR1 gets imported.

Off

On

NA

NA

Forwarded instantly

Forwarded instantly but TR2 will not be imported into the Production tenant until TR1 gets imported.

Off

Off

NA

NA

Forwarded as per Automation Settings / Manually

Forwarded as per Forward Automation Settings. On manually forwarding before TR1, the user will be prompted to forward the TR1 along with TR2 due to dependency. However, user can forward only TR2 by removing TR1 as dependency (i.e., by switching off the toggle, refer Fig 6) but TR2 will not be imported into the Production tenant until TR1 gets imported.


Fig 7 - Dependency Toggle for interdependent TRs

Please note that if the Production tenant is not provisioned then the user will not be able to see these 2 features since none of the TRs can be transported to the Production tenant i.e. the Test tenant will not have a target tenant. Hence, this will disable the need to have the Quality Assurance Approval and Instant Forward features.

Conclusion

Together, these features offer a robust framework for managing TRs, enhancing both control and efficiency in the deployment process. Users benefit from reduced manual oversight, streamlined workflows, and improved tenant reliability, ultimately leading to better management of enterprise resources and smoother operational processes.

Thank you for reading this blog post, hopefully, the blog post was informative and helped you to understand in detail how to use Quality Assurance Approval and Instant Forward features.

entry.title: Re: How to migrate Convergent Charging from Oracle DB to HANA DB.
entry.content: @janos_mucsi-besze Thank you for Kudos and comment!

entry.title: Can the SAP HANA database schema users be converted to "restricted user"
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I have the following three queries around HANA DB and SAP S/4 ECC/HANA:
1. Can the SAP HANA database schema users which SAP application uses to connect with HANA database be converted to "restricted user" as suggested in the article https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/f157e7b47b2a417a99eadd4b6c433b77/047d0187f27e46a19261c791a6e1d7a1.html?locale=en-US ?
2. If yes, will it mean that no one will be able to login to such schema users using SAP HANA studio or any other DB console while SAP will be able to operate normally, as it functioned before such change? i.e. the SAP application would still be able to access the database like earlier using such schema user accounts, but direct login access to such accounts would be blocked for other users
3. Which audit action(s) will log changes made to user types if configured in audit policies of HANA database?

SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA database SAP HANA

entry.title: Re: Extensibility options for Adobe Livecycle Designer in...

Hi Carl,

ALD and SAP have an agreement and the ALD we provide is supported and so far there are no plans to replace it.

 

In case of questions let me know.

 

Best regards

 

Didi

We have a requirement to use it in SAP BODS to load the data. Do you know if we can use this OData service to do so form BODS? If yes, do you have any reference on how can we use it?

If it is not possible, do you know any other FM/ BAPI which can be used with BODS to migrate the data?

Thanks and regards,
Het Patel

SAP Data Services

Dear Partner,

yes they do . Usually every month.

 

Best regards

 

Didi

entry.title: Dunning of Accounts Receivable Open Item from Residual Method of Partial Payment
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

Good day. I noticed that when the AR open item is a residual amount (remaining balance from partial payment using residual method), it is excluded from the dunning process via Create Dunning Notices app.  It just inherits the dunning level of the original invoice (with full amount) that was cleared during residual method partial payment posting.

Our client prefers the residual method because only the remaining balance floats as open item. Is there a way to make it subject for dunning?

Thank you very much.

Warm regards,

Cel

entry.title: Consolidation analysis Apps
entry.content:
Hi,

There are a number of apps due to be deleted from the Consolidation BCs (listed below), of which no successor app is mentioned nor these apps added to other business catalogs. These apps are;

Consolidated Balance Sheet
Cash Flow Statement
Interunit Reconciliation
Consolidated P&L by Function
Consolidated P&L by Nature
Data Analysis

Can you please update us with what the successor apps are please. This information is not provided in the Whats New download. 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Identifying right role with SAP ecosystem
entry.content:
I would take advantage of all the free training at learning.sap.com and then decide from there.  BTP is hot, along with the SAP Cloud products such as SAP Ariba and SAP Service Cloud.  Admin roles are very important in Cloud products.

In terms of product management, have you taken the free SAP Activate training at learning.sap.com? https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/discovering-sap-activate-implementation-tools-and-methodology

entry.title: OPD: Sending an E-Mail to the creator of a sales order, when all items are fully confirmed
entry.content:
Hello experts,

We are using OPD and are trying to implement a specific email dispatch scenario. A sales representative wants an email to be sent to the creator of a sales order whenever all items in the order are confirmed. Is this possible with OPD? If so, how can it be achieved? If not, what alternative approaches would you recommend?

Kind regards,
Tim

entry.title: Easy access to your SAP Easy Access Menu via App Finder
entry.content:
Many customers are wondering if it is possible in their Fiori Launchpad to leverage existing native SAP GUI for Windows transactions from their User Menu or SAP menu (SAP Easy Access menu) from the ABAP Back End without the need for complicated steps such as configuring new tiles. We are glad to announce that a very simple solution is available. This enables users to search for and launch any transactions from multiple ABAP backends they are authorized to use in order to combine the Fiori interface with native SAP GUI for Windows transactions.

 Home Page using the Horizon theme planned for Q4 2025

 

The following blog post describes how to acquire access to your SAP GUI for Windows transactions (also Web Dynpro Apps) via the App Finder. Once your transactions are displayed in the App Finder, you just “pin” them to your home page.*Version 8.10 is planned for Q4 2025

Note: In the “classic” SAP Business Client connection you can already start your work automatically with transaction SMEN. See the following Note:

Customer Services & Support Knowledge - How to default the SAP Easy Access menu | HCSM

Requirements

Software Component:

For NetWeaver 7.4+, SAP_UI 750 SP04+; for NetWeaver 7.31 or lower, UI Add-on 2.0 SP04+

Note: SAP Fiori Launchpad must be configured on the frontend system

 

Step-by-Step to your native SAP GUI for Windows transactions and Web Dynpro Apps

 

Configure Trust Relationship

  1. Assign Authorization S_RFCACL

Use transaction PFCG to create a role and assign authorization S_RFCACL to end users.

  1. Create RFC Connection from Backend to Frontend

Use transaction SM59 to create and configure the RFC destination.

  1. Define Frontend System as Trusted

Use transaction SMT1 to define the frontend system as trusted.

Configure RFC Connections and System Alias

  1. Create RFC Connection - ABAP Connection

Use transaction SM59 to create the RFC destination for ABAP connection.

  1. Create RFC Connection - HTTP(S) Connection

Use transaction SM59 to create the RFC destination for HTTP(S) connection.

  1. Create System Alias

Use transaction SPRO to navigate and create system aliases.

Activate OData Services

  1. Activate Services

Use transaction /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE to activate /UI2/EASY_ACCESS_MENU and /UI2/USER_MENU.

Configure Navigation

  1. Create Target Mapping

Use Fiori Launchpad Designer to create target mappings for transactions and Web Dynpro applications.

  1. Assign Authorization

Use transaction PFCG to create or modify roles and assign authorization to target mappings.

Test SAP Easy Access Menu on Fiori Launchpad

  1. Log in to Fiori Launchpad
  2. Open App Finder via the Fiori Launchpad Menu and verify User Menu and SAP Menu tabs to ensure they reflect the backend system menus.

Choose binoculars to open the App Finder

If the steps above are not detailed enough for you and you need to look some things up, check the following document providing as well many, many screenshots:

How to Enable SAP Easy Access Menu for Fiori Launchpad Step-by-Step

 Example for User Menu in App Finder

Select other systems to access transactions

If you have any further questions or suggestions, please let us know.

entry.title: Re: Freight Unit is not created automatically for Sales O...
entry.content:
Hi Dominik,

1.I have checked the TM integration using report#/SCMTMS/CHK_DOC_INT_TM_INTEGR, it is showing all steps fine but below two errors are coming. 

Document flow: No FU(s) assigned

All items have status 'Rejected' or 'No Standard Items'; freight units cannot be built 

2. Automatic freight building rule is already active and sales order don't have any blocks.

3.  integration based on actual quantities is already active in the logistics integration profile.

4. /SCMTMS/SUBSEQUENT_FUB_LOGINT  i have run this report but it is throwing below errors.

 Problem class Other

under message text

No freight units created

Freight units not created for Sales Order *****

entry.title: SF Incentive Management: Measurements per Classifier
entry.content:
Hello Community,

In a current project I have a challenge with the calculation of measurements. Perhaps you have ideas for solving this or have already had a similar requirement.

We are working with classifiers, e.g. customers, to assign transactions to sales reps. A sales rep can be assigned 1 to n customers. This assignment is realized via a territory that contains the customers (classifiers) and is assigned to the position of the sales rep.

To calculate a measurement, we now need to calculate the turnover that this sales representative has made with each individual customer. However, I can only use the territory in the conditions for the rule, which results in the sales being calculated for all assigned customers. Or I can explicitly specify the customers in the condition, but then I would have to create a rule for each existing customer, which is not practical in a productive scenario.

In our example, we used customers as classifiers, but this requirement could also be relevant for other classifiers, for example: sales per product or sales per zip code.

Currently my only idea is to solve this via a stored procedure in the database, but then I can only work with the aggregated final result of the calculation and cannot present the intermediate results to the user.

What would be the ideal way to solve this requirement?

Many thanks and best regards,

Maximilian

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK - push notifications and other issues
entry.content:
@alagar_pandian You won't get the actual source code of the branded MDK client generated in cloud build.

Using the cloud build you can (see this tutorial)

  1. Create a standard MDK client by providing your own app icon and app logo.
  2. Create a customized MDK client by importing your local .mdkproject similar to what you might have done via MDK SDK locally on your machine.

For #a, use step 1 to 2 from the push-customclient tutorial. 
For #b, use step 1 to 3

check out this solution that covers exactly what you need:

And we plan in 2025 some enhancements so that probably it gets much more easier.

Best regards

Volker

entry.title: Purchase Group as a condition in Purchase requisition workflow
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are configuring flexible workflow for Purchase requisition and unalbe to find the Purchase group as a Start condition. We have requirement to have the PR release based on Purchase groups but the same is not available in the Flexible workflow for PR in application 'Manage Workflows for Purchase Requisition'.

Could you please check and help us on how to configure the Flexible workflow based on the Purchase groups for PR's.

Regards.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Purchase Group as a condition in Purchase requisition...
entry.content:
There are 2 BADIs to incorporate new conditions ,

You can find  the BADIS in the Flexible Workflow Framework:

BADi - SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_DEF,  Method  : GET_CONDITIONS

Use : to define additional conditions for scenarios

BADi - SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_EVAL,  Method  : EVALUATE_CONDITION

Use : to evaluate the additional conditions defined

you can find sample code in the following blogs and KBAS :

 

https://blogs.sap.com/2019/12/03/sap-s-4hana-cloud-flexible-workflow-with-new-custom-pre-condition-for-purchase-requisition/

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/sap-s-4hana-cloud-flexible-workflow-with-new-custom-pre-condition-for/ba-p/13458649

https://me.sap.com/notes/3331439/E

https://me.sap.com/notes/2841783/E

https://me.sap.com/notes/2767845/E

 

Thanks 

SAP Tehcnical Support

entry.title: Re: iOS Builds Fail to Export
entry.content:
Hi @Fadhilah 

Did you found any solution on this problem?

entry.title: SAP Treasury and Risk Management Loan/Deposits, Interest Accrual Calculation
entry.content:
Dear colleagues,

let's assume we have a very easy loan contract.

Start Date 01.05. , end date 01.07.

Loan value: 7.000.000 EUR, paid by the end of the contract on 01.07.
Interest rate 10 %

When we post the accrual for the key date 31.05. the system is calculating 7.000.000 * 0,1 / 12 = 58.333 EUR, which is correct.

The problem comes with the second accrual. Here the system is calculating the cumulated accrual which is 116.666 EUR as it's calculated for May and June. 
We would like to have only the value of the period June as the accrual posting which is 58.333 EUR aswell. That means the system should check when the last accrual was posted and should only post the outstanding value till the key date (for example 30.06.2024).

 

Do you know any possibility to avoid the cumulative accruals?

Thanks so much and best regards,

Marcel 

SAP Treasury and Risk Management Treasury Management

entry.title: 10+ ways to reshape your SAP landscape with SAP Business Technology
entry.content:
Blog 10: Reference Solution Architecture

Introduction

The following chapter focuses on reference architectures for Software Solutions consisting of applications and services which are provided as solution templates for typical end-to-end scenarios (e.g. Reference Solution Architecture for the Application Architecture domain). These end-to-end scenarios can be clustered in solution domains due to common sets of solution components that fulfill the given scenario.

Software vendors recommend reference architectures for typical scenarios based on their solution portfolio. These recommendations are expected to be tested and proven in practice (e.g. proven by existing customer references).

The re-use of a proven reference solution accelerates the delivery and reduces implementation risk. Support by SW vendors and their partner network is high in comparison to custom implementations what offers the following benefits [Source: Reference Architecture on Wikipedia]:

  • Use of standard solutions ensures broader support, available skills, documentation and information and easier governance (common understanding of the solution).
  • Cost reduction due to potential re-use of solution components, skills and processes and unrequired custom software development.

SAP provides Reference Solution Architectures on different levels of abstraction:

  • The SAP Enterprise Architecture Framework includes SAP’s Reference Business Architecture (RBA) and Reference Solution Architecture (RSA) where the latter shows which highlevel Solution Components from the SAP SW portfolio enable which business process and business capability in the Business Architecture (see Blog).
  • A more detailed view on SAP RSA is provided in the Business Process description of the SAP Business Accelerator Hub. Here, specific solution components, solution processes and solution data flows are visualized for selected SAP end-to-end processes and process variants, for example for Lead-to-Cash for Subscription and Usage Business for Cloud Deployment.
  • Customer specific solutions can be built using applications and services on the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). For this, BTP solution templates are provided for particular technology domains such as integration, extensions, data and analytics. The provided BTP Reference Solution Architectures describe the interaction of BTP services with each other and with business applications from SAP, partners or third party. These Reference Architectures address selected use cases and scenarios clustered by Architecture Patterns (see next chapter).

SAP BTP Reference Solution Architectures

Last year we launched the availability of the "reference architecture" capability in the SAP Discovery Center to provide multiple reference architectures for different SAP BTP categories, please check also the blog for more details.

In the architecture catalog you can easily search for a reference architecture or select one of the categories:

  • Application Development
  • Automation
  • Data and Analytics
  • Hyperscaler
  • Integration 
  • Security

To get more details about a specific architecture, you can easily select the tile which already provide a first overview. Once the you’ve clicked the reference architecture the "SAP BTP Solution Diagram" as architecture diagram is shown on the top.

In the detailed description you find the explantion of the reference architecture and additional links to further missions and SAP BTP services. 

In case you want to learn more about the topic of SAP BTP reference architectures, please check also the SAP BTP Guidance Framework.

How to use examples from the SAP BTP Solution Diagram repository to craft your own

With the release of the "new" BTP Solution Diagram repository as open source (free of charge) in March 2024 we provide also examples which helps you to create or reuse SAP BTP Solution Diagrams by using the diagramming tools draw.io or Lucidchart.

For Lucidchart we provide now also the SAP BTP Service Icons integration which can be connected to your account.

The easiest way to make use of this SAP BTP Solution Diagram examples is to clone the "SAP BTP Solution Diagram" repository on your local device via the GitHuB CLI or by using for example Visual Studio Code which also provides a marketplace extension for draw.io. 

Another option is directly use the examples in draw.io desktop with the option to make changes to one of the BTP Solution Diagrams by using the official BTP Service Icons for draw.io (desktop) :

In addition to the desktop version you can also make use of the web based editor of draw.io, we're happy to share now the newest update !

With version 24.7.0 for the web based editor the SAP Service Icons and shapes are added as "standard" to draw.io !

Integration into the bigger picture

A large challenge of Enterprises is the creation and maintenance of the existing IT landscape documentation. Enterprise Architecture Management tools help managing this, especially when features like Landscape Discovery are provided.

SAP LeanIX EAM is an example of an Enterprise Architecture Management tool. It provides a 360° overview of all your applications, business capabilities, and IT components so you can gain control of your expanding IT landscape and create roadmaps for major transformation initiatives.

With the release of SAP Landscape Discovery you can view all your SAP applications and SAP BTP services to identify your baseline architecture (see Introducing SAP Landscape Discovery in SAP LeanIX). By the end of the year, you will also be able to view and link extensions which you have been built on SAP BTP (see Sapphire innovation keynote, https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4DP4RpPRlhQ).

This means that any solution implementation documentation can also be maintained in LeanIX by using its Diagram feature. Linking the LeanIX Fact Sheets of the used solution components adds more information to the diagram for example information about the component provider and roadmap as well as additional customer-managed information like lifecycle and cost.

The BTP Reference Solution Architecture drawings from SAP could be directly used as template for these type of diagrams with custom implementations. The linkage to the solution components ensures existence and re-use of the required applications and components and helps identifying additionally required licenses.

For completeness, the BTP account structure can also be reflected in the Solution Diagram. This helps making solutions on the BTP transparent on Enterprise Architecture level and, therefore, ensures a seamless integration in the documentation of the Corporate Enterprise Architecture for maintaining a single source of truth of it.

This blog is co-authored by Stefan

entry.title: How to Create Deeplinks by using Specific Tab URL in ODE Stories?
entry.content:
I am seeking support  with something is so basic but there is no clear guidance.

I would like to use hyperlinks or Deeplink in the e-mails  or MS Office file to link tabs in specific stories.  The issue is in the ODE(Optimized Design Experience) stories,  tabs don't have specific URL every tab  has same URL within the stories. Therefore I can not create deeplinks.

The right question would be how can I get the actual URL of tabs within the a story. In this way I am able to create deeplinks in e-mail or other documents.

It would be great, if you can help.

Best Regards.

#SAC #OptimizedDesignExperience #Hyperlink #Deeplink #Story

Ali

entry.title: Re: Can the SAP HANA database schema users be converted t...
entry.content:
HANA DB schema users can be converted normally to "restricted users" with limited privileges, intended for client application access, but not full SQL console access.
Converting schema users to restricted users prevents login via SAP HANA studio or other DB consoles, but should doesn't affect SAP application's normal operation and database access.
The "ALTER USER" audit action logs user type changes in HANA database, configurable in the audit policies.

entry.title: Re: EWM fixed bin + capacity check during putaway
entry.content:
I'm facing the same issue. I've setup the capacity check in the storage type = 2 = Packaging material, the packaging material has a capacity consumption of 10 and the bin a capacity of 40. I've stored 3 pallets on the bin. On the bin there is capacity usage = 0. What is wrong here? Why SAP doesn't calculate any capacity usage?

=> Problem solved. The HUs were created before the packaging material was updated. I repacked the product to a new HU and now the HU has a capacity consumption.

entry.title: Re: Be Comfortable, Authentic and Interview the Interviewer 🧑🏻‍💻
entry.content: Thanks for sharing Kunal!

entry.title: Re: HANA engine depend on which factors ?
entry.content: The HANA engine's performance relies on hardware, software, network, configuration, and database factors. It needs specific hardware, compatible software, a stable network, correct configuration, and a well-managed database. Your SAP Control Center issue might be due to network misconfiguration after changing the DB IP. Update the network settings in the SAP Control Center to match the new DB IP.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA aATP Supply creation-Based Confirmation (SBC) Series Part 3 - Simple Use Case
entry.content:
Hi kovacsan,

Thanks for sharing this nice article, very useful.

I have a question - When you create Avail.check group 'DS' , is it just an entry that's created or copied from any existing entry like DR, CH etc.,. ?  since its a check group, do I need to have any dependent settings for DS to be made?

Thanks,

Kishor

entry.title: Configuring Standard API in S/4 HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Generating OData of standard API :

First we have to login to SAP business accelerator hub account and go to SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public edition.

 

Choose a standard API as shown below

 

 

 In this case I have choose the product master standard API.

 

Select the overview tab, under that we can see Communication Scenario ID.

 

 

Copy communication scenario from standard API. Now Move to the

 S/4Hana cloud system -> Communication Arrangements

Select the Option New, and add a Scenario ID which we have copied from the Standard API

 

Create new communication system:

Search for Communication System in S/4 HANA cloud, here we need to create user and password for all OData services

Click on New, 

System will ask for new communication system id and system name. we can give the ID & Name of our choice. And Click on create. 

Give the Host name as shown below,  

In User for Inbound Communication, click on Add and Create New Inbound Communication User. Click on “New User” 

Give the username and description and select on proposed password to get the password which will give by system itself. (Copy the user name and password and paste somewhere so that, Once the user and password has created we can use the same for all the OData service Consumption.) 

Click on create 

Click on Save  

Then add your custom communication system in this communication arrangement

Then you get required ODATA URL

Now will connect S/4HANA Cloud to Power BI or any other reporting tools through OData Feed. Example:Open Power BI->get Data->OData feed.

In the OData feed enter the OData URL generated in S/4HANA Cloud. And give user name and password that we have generated in S4hana cloud “Communication System” to get the OData url.

Then Click on connect.

Select the checkbox to data preview and click on Transform data or Load.

entry.title: Re: SAP Certification Transformation: How You Can Get and Stay Certified
entry.content: Hi.. I have some experience in SAP C4C functional version 1 and now preparing for version 2, I just need some questions to prepare for version 2 certification and where can I find it? Thanks in advance.

entry.content:
Hi Markus,

Is the issue still there? If the VA12 is behaved as you described, I would suggest you create a ticket, with which the SAP Support expert could check this issue in your system.

Best Regards,

Sean

entry.title: Display of page map fails
entry.content:
Hi,

When trying to open the page map for my application I get only the following result:

In addition I get the error message 'EntryNotFound (FileSystemError): ftfs:/c:/Users/I023412/CALM/ptm-lib-ui/src/app.json'.
The application information is 'all green':

I wonder whether the problem is not due to the fact that project contains only the frontend of the application, i.e. the cap based odata v4 backend is implemented in a separate project. On the other hand, the application can be previewed without problem which makes me thing that this is a technical issue with page map application.

Best regards,

Axel.

P.S.: As the problem seems to be related to the manifest.json, I attach this file to the post.

entry.title: Re: Configuration run in sales order
entry.content: If I'm not wrong, SAP doesn't have a built-in feature for automatic configuration or costing runs in sales orders. But, may you can use custom programs, User Exit or BADI for this.

entry.title: Re: Configuring Standard API in S/4 HANA Cloud
entry.content: Hi if anyone has any doubts feel free to reply

entry.title: Re: Dispatcher does not start after Refresh database
entry.content:
The dispatcher can't connect to the database due to possible network issues, wrong database parameters, or a non-running database service.

To troubleshoot, please check below:
1. Verify network connectivity between application and database servers.
2. Confirm the database service is running.
3. Check the SAP profile's database parameters for accuracy.
4. Review database logs for errors.
5. Examine SAP startup logs for errors.

entry.title: Re: Not possible to display SAC Live BW Query Texts in Ge...
entry.content: Hi , for Translation of SAC Content check: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/translation-of-sap-analytics-cloud-content-in-multiple-language/ba-p/13524089

entry.title: Re: Error MLCCS 202 in VFX3
entry.content: Hello , Thank you for your information, but I am using SAP ECC and this note does not apply. I also tried other notes like 2038713 without success, any additional information could be useful. Thanks

entry.title: Cancelled custom Invoice also coming in MIRO
entry.content:
Dear All,

I have created custom PO, after that I ahve posted custom MIRO, then MIGO also done.

After that I cancelled MIGO and custom MIRO also, and again when I am doing custom MIRO system picking double line for custom MIRO and social wlf charges.

in PO condition I have JCDB 10% and ZCWM 10% but at the time of miro system Picking two line for custom duty and two line for social wlf charges.

entry.title: Re: Periodic billing of service products
entry.content:
Hi @Sebastian_Spies,

In the S/4HANA Public Cloud, the sales order only supports the milestone billing plan for the solution of Sales Order Processing with Customer Down Payment (BKJ) - Process Navigator - SAP for Me. 

For your case with the service product, please check and consider the Service Contract Management (3MO) - Process Navigator - SAP for Me and/or Recurring Services (4X5) - Process Navigator - SAP for Me, in which service contract enables the periodic billing plan. 

Best regards,

Troy

entry.title: Re: How to Reclaim size of volume "/hana/shared and /hana...
entry.content:
Please check:
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/how-to-reclaim-size-of-volume-quot-hana-log-quot-when-it-is-full/ba-p/13404860
https://me.sap.com/notes/0002988616

entry.title: Re: Half/full pallet order quantity prerequisite for sale...
entry.content: It's a duplicate question, I would suggest to close this one, while let's track the other one: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/half-full-pallet-order-quantity-prerequisite-for-sales-order-creation/qaq-p/13713698

entry.title: Re: Maintenance Plan Call Scheduling type/status
entry.content:
Hi,

Please check MHIS table.

When a call is manually made, an entry of type M is recorded. If the call is scheduled, an entry of type T is recorded. An entry of type N is made for a new start or restart. When a plan starts in cycle, an entry of type Z is created.

Regards

entry.title: How do we move stock from non-EWM managed plant to EWM managed plant belonging to same company code?
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

A query. How do we move stock from non-EWM managed plant to EWM managed plant belonging to same company code (both integrated with TM)? What are the required configurations for that?

Regards

Bharath

entry.title: Re: Recognition issues for projects even if "Enablement Assistant" is enabled
entry.content: Hello, I know registries are at user level. Do you know if those registries can be applied at device level?

entry.title: Re: RAP directly navigate to List or next position after...
entry.content:
@itp_mka 

It seems that your requirements may not be achievable with Fiori elements, but how about using the Flexible Column Layout? By opening the List Report on one side and the Object Page on the other side, you can simply click on the next row in the List Report after finishing editing, without needing to press the back button.

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/developing-an-sap-fiori-elements-app-based-on-a-cap-odata-v4-service/configuring-the-flexible-column-layout_b5066855-aa7c-4943-a64b-64050ccfea75

entry.title: adobe form
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I am doing initial research on adobe form.

Do we have any standard forms available for below forms.

bulk invoice print out
BIO Domestic/GTR/Export Invoice Printout
BII Invoice Print out

Let me know if you have designed any of this form.

Regards,

JP

entry.title: SAC Script needed to retrive the selected values of a Hierachie filter parameter/variable
entry.content:
SAC Script needed to retrive the selected values of a Hierachy filter parameter/variable

-got a BW Query,
-the BW Query got a Companycode with a Hierarchy,
- the Companycode Hierachy is for example:
Country Companycode
----------+---------------------
DE        2150
DE        2222
DE       .........
AT         8010
AT        8020
AT        .....
...
REST  1001
REST  1002
REST  ....


The query got a Customer Exit Hierachy Node Variable ZHIERNODEMULTI
for multiple values.

I need  to get the values of selected nodes of the customer exit varable ZHIERNODEMULTI

in a script:
What means in the sample the values of the 0HIERODE :  AT &  DE. 

Anybody got a fitting script ?

Thank You !

Best Martin

entry.title: Re: Can the SAP HANA database schema users be converted t...
entry.content:
Thank you for the quick response. However, the concern was mainly due to the SAP help article https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/b3ee5778bc2e4a089d3299b82ec762a7/dec8d273bb571014b4c2b771d3e0f166.html?version=2.0.04

It states that the restricted user cannot do the following:

  • They cannot create objects in the database as they are not authorized to create objects in their own database schema.
  • They cannot view any data in the database as they are not granted the standard PUBLIC role.

Won't the SAP application need these in order to conduct daily operations?

entry.title: Data Source and Service Selection configuration in SAP Business Application Studio
entry.content:
Hello,

I am doing the exercise Creating an analytical list page of the course "Developing and Extending SAP Fiori Elements Apps". I don't know how to complete the Data Source and Service Selection in SAP Business Application Studio.

I've completed the previous exercises. According to the information provided in the Git repository, I am supposed to get "abap-cloud-default_abap-trial" as an option for "System":

Based on the information provided in the Git repository, I assume this option means connecting to the back end I've used earlier in this course. Is the back end connected with my account in SAP BTP?

In Eclipse, I needed to provide a service key and to log in to connect the back end, in VSCode, I needed only to provide a service key, and in SAP BAS, I don't see a possibility to provide a service key.

Why do I not get the option "abap-cloud-default_abap-trial" for "System"?

Best regards,

Alexander Schädlich
